name: Western Union
description_short: South San Francisco's Western Union at 30 Chestnut Ave offers check cashing and money transfer services daily.
description_long:
The Western Union location at 30 Chestnut Ave in South San Francisco operates as a standalone check-cashing and money transfer service. This South San Francisco branch is open seven days a week from 8AM to 7PM, making it convenient for local residents and workers who need weekend access. The store provides direct service for financial transactions without requiring a separate host bank location.

At this South San Francisco, CA location, you can cash checks, send and receive domestic and international money transfers, and pay bills. The staff can assist with wire transfers and other financial services. Contact the store directly at +1 650-952-8867 to confirm specific services or processing times.

South San Francisco residents and commuters working in the Bay Area can rely on this 30 Chestnut Ave location for quick financial transactions. Bring a valid government-issued ID for check cashing and money transfer services. Western Union is a trusted payment service for sending money domestically and internationally.
address: 30 Chestnut Ave, South San Francisco, CA 94080

company_info.headquarters: 30 Chestnut Ave, South San Francisco, CA 94080

pros: Extensive U.S. agent network with 150+ locations in Colorado alone, including major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, and Circle K
24/7 online money transfer initiation through WU.com regardless of agent location hours
Same-day cash pickup available at thousands of retail agent locations nationwide
Multilingual platform support in six languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Extended operating hours at many locations (open until 8-11 PM) providing after-hours service accessibility
Comprehensive service suite including money orders, bill payments, mobile top-ups, and inmate payments
Western Union Rewards loyalty program and mobile app for transaction tracking and management
cons: Transfer fees and exchange rates are typically higher than online-only competitors and some bank wire services
Service availability and hours vary significantly by agent location—some locations offer money orders only
No geolocation functionality on website when browser geolocation is disabled, limiting location search convenience
Limited control over service quality and customer experience as it depends on individual retail agent training and staffing
Temporarily unavailable payment methods (Orange wallet issue noted on website) indicate occasional service disruptions
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts needing accessible cash transfer services
International immigrants and migrant workers sending remittances to family members overseas
Consumers in rural or underserved areas relying on retail agent locations for financial services
Customers who value convenience of extended hours and multiple location options over lowest-cost transfers
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers without traditional bank access who need reliable, accessible money transfer and cash services, particularly in underserved communities. The primary caveat is that fees and exchange rates are substantially higher than digital-only competitors, making it important to compare costs before transferring larger amounts.
services: Delivery method updates for received funds
meta_description: Western Union at 30 Chestnut Ave, South San Francisco, CA. Daily check cashing and money transfer services.
